title: School terms and holidays dates - Ministry of Education
content:
Ngā rā o ngā wāhanga ā-kura me ngā hararei

## School terms and holidays dates
Term and holiday dates for state schools, kura and state-integrated schools to support school boards, leaders and staff and families to plan their calendars.
- 2026 school holidays
- 2027 school holidays
- 2028 school holidays
- Why schools have different term dates
- When schools will close
- Early childhood services and private schools
Holidays and mandatory half-days are statutory requirements for state and state-integrated schools and kura.

### Term 1 (up to 10 weeks) #
Starts between Monday 26 January and Monday 9 February and ends Thursday 2 April 2026 (78-96 half days).
Public holiday: Waitangi Day – Friday 6 February.
Monday 20 April to Friday 3 July 2026 (106 half days).
Public holidays: Anzac Day – Saturday 25 April (Anzac Day observed on Monday 27 April) and King’s Birthday – Monday 1 June.
Monday 20 July to Friday 25 September 2026 (100 half days).

### Term 4 (up to 10 weeks) #
Monday 12 October to no later than Friday 18 December 2026 (up to 98 half days).
Public holiday: Labour Day – Monday 26 October.

### Half-day opening requirement 2026 #
Primary, intermediate and specialist schools must be open for instruction for a minimum of 378 half days in 2026.
Secondary and composite schools must be open for instruction for a minimum of 376 half days in 2026.
Schools have flexibility to choose the start and end dates of their school year, so long as they start between Monday 26 January and Monday 9 February, and end no later than 18 December 2026.

### Unused curriculum half-days #
If schools do not use the curriculum half-days granted by the Minister of Education, the minimum half-day requirement reverts to:
- 382 for primary, intermediate and specialist schools
- 380 for secondary and composite schools.

## 2026 school holidays #
Friday 3 April to Sunday 19 April 2026.
Public holidays: Good Friday 3 April, Easter Monday 6 April, Easter Tuesday 7 April (a school holiday).
Saturday 4 July to Sunday 19 July 2026.
Public holiday: Matariki – Friday 10 July.
Saturday 26 September to Sunday 11 October 2026.
Start no later than Saturday 19 December 2026 and run for 5 or 6 weeks (from school's closing date until opening date of school the following year).

### Term 1 (up to 11 weeks) #
Starts between Thursday 28 January and Wednesday 3 February and ends Friday 9 April 2027 (88–96 half days).
Public holidays: Waitangi Day – Saturday 6 February observed on Monday 8 February, Good Friday – 26 March, Easter Monday – 29 March, Easter Tuesday – 30 March (a school holiday).
Tuesday 27 April to Friday 2 July 2027 (94 half days).
Public holidays: King’s Birthday – Monday 7 June, Matariki – Friday 25 June.
Monday 19 July to Friday 24 September 2027 (100 half days).

### Term 4 (up to 10 weeks) #
Monday 11 October to no later than Friday 17 December 2027 (up to 98 half days).
Public holiday: Labour Day – Monday 25 October.

### Half-day opening requirement 2027 #
Primary, intermediate and specialist schools are required to be open for instruction for a minimum of 376 half days in 2027.
Secondary and composite schools are required to be open for instruction for minimum of 376 half days in 2027.
Schools have flexibility to choose the start and end dates of their school year, so long as they start between Thursday 28 January and Wednesday 3 February, and end no later than 17 December 2027.

### Unused curriculum half-days #
If schools do not use the curriculum half-days granted by the Minister of Education, the minimum half-day requirement reverts to:
- 380 for primary, intermediate and specialist schools
- 380 for secondary and composite schools.

## 2027 school holidays #
Saturday 10 April to Monday 26 April 2027.
Public holiday: Anzac Day observed on Monday 26 April.
Saturday 3 July to Sunday 18 July 2027.
Saturday 25 September to Sunday 10 October 2027.
Start no later than Saturday 18 December 2027 and run for 5 or 6 weeks (from school's closing date until opening date of school the following year).

### Term 1 (up to 11 weeks) #
Starts between Monday 31 January and Tuesday 8 February and ends Thursday 13 April 2028 (96-106 half days).
Public holidays: Waitangi Day – Sunday 6 February observed on Monday 7 February.
Monday 1 May to Friday 7 July 2028 (98 half days).
Public holidays: King’s Birthday – Monday 5 June.
Monday 24 July to Friday 29 September 2028 (100 half days).

### Term 4 (up to 9 weeks) #
Monday 16 October to no later than Friday 15 December 2028 (up to 88 half days).
Public holiday: Labour Day – Monday 23 October.

### Half-day opening requirement 2028 #
Primary, intermediate and specialist schools are required to be open for instruction for a minimum of 382 half days in 2028.
Secondary and composite schools are required to be open for instruction for a minimum of 380 half days in 2028.
Schools have flexibility to choose the start and end dates of their school year, so long as they start between Monday 31 January and Tuesday 8 February, and end no later than 15 December 2028.

## 2028 school holidays #
Friday 14 April to Sunday 30 April 2028.
Public holiday: Good Friday – 14 April, Easter Monday – 17 April, Easter Tuesday – 18 April (a school holiday), Anzac Day – Tuesday 25 April.
Saturday 8 July to Sunday 23 July 2028.
Public holiday: Matariki – Friday 14 July.
Saturday 30 September to Sunday 15 October 2028.
Start no later than Saturday 16 December 2028 and run for 5 or 6 weeks (from school's closing date until opening date of school the following year).

## Cohort entry dates #
Find out about the 2025-2026 and 2027-2028 dates for cohort entry.

## Why schools have different term dates #
Schools and kura kaupapa Māori have some flexibility to decide when Term 1 starts, and when Term 4 finishes. Terms 2 and 3 are fixed.
Where possible, we align the end of the first term with the Easter holidays. This can make Term 1 shorter or longer depending on when Easter falls.
There is a set amount of teaching time that schools must be open for each year. Having flexible dates allows each school to meet this requirement and accommodate local events and teacher-only days.
Secondary schools need to open for slightly fewer days per year than primary and intermediate schools. They may close earlier at the end of the year.

## When schools will close #
During a school term, schools will close for:
- Saturdays and Sundays
- public holidays and local anniversary days
- the Tuesday following Easter Monday
- any emergency that requires the school to close.
Schools might also choose to close for local events like gala and show days, or for religious holidays in some communities.
Public holidays and anniversary dates – Employment New Zealand

### If you're not happy with your school's term dates #
We encourage schools to align term dates with other local schools where possible. If your family is impacted by variation in term dates between local schools, you can talk to your principal.

## Early childhood services and private schools #
Some early childhood services choose to follow the school terms and holidays. Full-time daycare services are usually open through the school holidays but close over Christmas. Check with your service.
Private schools can have different terms and holidays. They can also open on Easter Tuesday. Check with the school or service.

#### Term 1 (up to 11 weeks)
Starts between Monday 27 January and Friday 7 February and ends Friday 11 April 2025 (92-108 half days).
Public holiday: Waitangi – Thursday 6 February.
Monday 28 April to Friday 27 June 2025 (86 half days).
Public holidays: King’s Birthday – Monday 2 June and Matariki Friday – 20 June.
Monday 14 July to Friday 19 September 2025 (100 half days).

#### Term 4 (up to 11 weeks)
Monday 6 October to no later than Friday 19 December 2025 (up to 108 half days).
Public holiday: Labour Day – Monday 27 October.

#### Half-day opening requirement 2025
Primary, intermediate and specialist schools must be open for instruction for a minimum of 382 half days in 2025.
Secondary and composite schools must be open for instruction for a minimum of 376 half days in 2025.
Schools have flexibility to choose the start and end dates of their school year, so long as they start between Monday 27 January and Friday 7 February, and end no later than 19 December 2025.

### 2025 school holidays #
Saturday 12 April to Sunday 27 April 2025.
Public holidays: Good Friday 18 April, Easter Monday 21 April, Easter Tuesday 22 April (a school holiday) and Anzac Day – Friday 25 April.
Saturday 28 June to Sunday 13 July 2025.
Saturday 20 September to Sunday 5 October 2025.
Start no later than Saturday 20 December 2025 and run for 5 or 6 weeks (from school's closing date until opening date of school the following year).
